executive_summary: Store delivered weekly sales of 343,756.41 inc VAT (314,457.62 ex VAT) for the week ending 13 June 2026.
Overall margin of 18.05% is recorded for the week; this figure warrants monitoring given several departments are reporting incomplete P&L data, making full margin validation difficult.
Total waste of 8,343.32 represents a waste percentage of 2.65% of sales ex VAT, which is a material cost risk and the single largest controllable drag on profitability this week.

Deli and Provisions are the standout departments in terms of reported profitability, both showing scan margins above 40% and 21% respectively with reported profit figures exceeding sales due to closing stock treatment.

The entire Fresh Meat category (Beef, Lamb, Pork, Bacon, Poultry, Fish) is reporting negative cost of sales figures, indicating that no sales, purchases, or opening stock data has been captured — these departments cannot be accurately assessed this week and require urgent investigation.

Waste is heavily concentrated in Produce (1,937.44), Deli (997.51), Provisions (990.20), and Meat (1,427.67 combined), together accounting for approximately 6,353 of the total 8,343.32 waste — over 76% of all waste.
Cigarettes and Produce are both reporting 100% margin, which is a data anomaly — COGS data is absent for these departments and the figures should not be taken at face value.

The department P&L total shows purchases and opening stock both at zero, which confirms significant data gaps across multiple departments this week; management should validate source data before using these figures for trading decisions.
biggest_wins: Deli reported a scan margin of 40.78%, the strongest theoretical margin in the store, and posted a reported profit of 12,943.03 against sales of 12,736.82 — reflecting strong pricing and category management, though closing stock treatment inflates the headline profit figure.
Provisions delivered sales of 20,757.87 with a scan margin of 21.63% and a reported profit of 20,966.78, indicating solid underlying performance in the category.
Produce generated sales of 20,710.96, the second highest sales volume among departments with recorded figures, with a scan margin of 19.58%, demonstrating good turnover for the category.
Cigarettes produced sales of 13,131.22 with a scan margin of 12.29%, consistent with the low-margin, high-volume nature of the category and no waste reported.
biggest_risks: Beef, Lamb, Pork, Bacon, Poultry, and Fish are all reporting negative cost of sales figures derived solely from closing stock values, with no sales, purchases, or opening stock recorded — these departments are effectively invisible from a P&L perspective this week and the data must be investigated and corrected.

Total waste of 8,343.32 at 2.65% of sales is a significant profitability risk. Meat waste alone totals 1,427.67 (Beef 270.20, Lamb 340.34, Pork 75.64, Bacon 80.98, Poultry 619.44, Fish 313.09), and given the absence of sales data for these departments, the waste-to-sales ratio in Fresh Meat is likely far worse than the store average.
Produce waste of 1,937.44 is the single largest waste line in the store. With Produce scan margin at 19.58%, this level of waste materially erodes what should be a profitable fresh category.
Deli waste of 997.51 is high relative to department sales of 12,736.82, representing approximately 7.8% waste against Deli sales — well above an acceptable threshold for a prepared foods environment.

Provisions waste of 990.20 against sales of 20,757.87 equates to approximately 4.8% waste-to-sales for the department, which is elevated and needs to be addressed through tighter ordering and rotation discipline.

Bread and Cakes waste of 705.08 is a notable line and suggests either overordering, insufficient markdown activity, or both — this category requires immediate attention to ordering patterns and end-of-day clearance processes.

The absence of complete purchase and opening stock data across multiple departments means the overall margin of 18.05% cannot be fully validated this week. Any decision-making based on these department-level figures carries data integrity risk.

Baby and Kids waste of 106.24 and Personal Care waste of 140.00 are unusual for ambient or near-ambient categories and should be reviewed to confirm these are genuine write-offs and not data entry errors.
department_callouts: Cigarettes: Sales of 13,131.22 recorded with scan margin of 12.29%. No COGS data available; reported 100% margin is a data artifact. No waste recorded. Category performing to expected low-margin, high-volume profile.

Beef: No sales or purchases data recorded. Closing stock of 3,285.50 generates a negative COS of -3,285.50. Waste of 270.20 recorded. P&L data is incomplete and must be investigated before any trading conclusions can be drawn.

Lamb: No sales or purchases data recorded. Closing stock of 472.41 generates a negative COS of -472.41. Waste of 340.34 is disproportionately high relative to the closing stock value and requires immediate review of ordering and rotation.
Pork: No sales or purchases data recorded. Closing stock of 357.95 generates a negative COS of -357.95. Waste of 75.64 recorded. Data gaps prevent meaningful analysis.
Bacon and Loose SRP: No sales or purchases data recorded. Closing stock of 1,615.75 generates a negative COS of -1,615.75. Waste of 80.98 recorded. Full P&L data required.

Poultry: No sales or purchases data recorded. Closing stock of 3,024.61 generates a negative COS of -3,024.61. Waste of 619.44 is the highest within the meat category and is a significant concern — this must be reviewed urgently against order quantities and sell-through rates.

Fish: No sales or purchases data recorded. Closing stock of 1,248.43 generates a negative COS of -1,248.43. Waste of 313.09 is material for a department with no visible sales figure. Ordering and waste controls must be reviewed.
Produce: Sales of 20,710.96 with scan margin of 19.58%. No COGS data; reported 100% margin is a data artifact. Waste of 1,937.44 is the highest in the store and the primary waste priority this week.

Deli: Sales of 12,736.82 with scan margin of 40.78% — the strongest theoretical margin in the store. Closing stock of 206.21. Waste of 997.51 is high at approximately 7.8% of department sales and must be addressed through production planning and portion control.

Provisions: Sales of 20,757.87 with scan margin of 21.63%. Closing stock of 208.91. Waste of 990.20 at approximately 4.8% of department sales is above target and requires review of ordering cycles and date management.
Grocery Impulse, Grocery Edible, Grocery Non-Food: Combined waste of 154.08 across these ambient lines is relatively low and broadly acceptable, though worth monitoring for trends.
Dairy: Waste of 185.42 recorded. No department-level P&L data provided; performance cannot be assessed beyond the waste figure.

Bread and Cakes: Waste of 705.08 recorded with no corresponding P&L data. This is a significant waste figure for the category and ordering patterns along with markdown utilisation should be reviewed immediately.
action_plan: Investigate and correct the missing sales, purchases, and opening stock data for Beef, Lamb, Pork, Bacon, Poultry, and Fish immediately — without complete data, the store P&L is unreliable and trading decisions in Fresh Meat cannot be made with confidence.
Confirm why Cigarettes and Produce are showing 100% margin with zero COGS — validate that purchase and opening stock data is feeding correctly into the P&L system for these departments.

Conduct an urgent review of Produce ordering and waste management. Waste of 1,937.44 in a single week is not sustainable at a 19.58% scan margin. Implement daily waste counts, review order quantities by line, and ensure markdown activity is being executed on short-dated stock.

Review Poultry waste of 619.44 as a priority within the Fresh Meat category. Establish whether this is driven by overordering, poor rotation, or insufficient markdown of short-dated product, and adjust the weekly order accordingly.

Address Deli waste of 997.51 through a review of daily production schedules. Production should be aligned to sales patterns by time of day, and any unsold prepared product should be evaluated for markdown or disposal earlier in the trading day to reduce full write-off.

Review Provisions waste of 990.20 against sales of 20,757.87. Identify the specific lines driving waste and adjust order frequency or quantities. Ensure FIFO rotation is being applied consistently across all chilled Provisions lines.

Implement an immediate review of Bread and Cakes ordering in light of waste of 705.08. Engage with the supplier or bakery partner to establish whether daily order quantities are aligned to recent sales history, and ensure end-of-day markdown procedures are being followed.

Investigate the source of Baby and Kids waste of 106.24 and Personal Care waste of 140.00 — confirm these are genuine product write-offs and not system or scanning errors, as these figures are unusually high for non-perishable or near-ambient categories.

Schedule a department managers meeting to review the overall waste figure of 8,343.32 (2.65% of sales). Set individual department waste targets for the coming week and establish a daily waste reporting process to track progress in real time.

Ensure all department managers are completing opening stock counts and recording purchases accurately each week so that COGS can be calculated for every department — incomplete data is preventing effective margin management across Fresh Meat, Produce, and Cigarettes.

Review Fish waste of 313.09 against Fish closing stock of 1,248.43 — this ratio suggests a structural issue with sell-through in the category. Consider reducing order quantities and reviewing the range for slower-moving lines.

Carry forward a formal waste review for Lamb, where waste of 340.34 is high relative to a closing stock of only 472.41 — this indicates that a significant proportion of stock received during the week was written off rather than sold.
